title: The Brain-Gut Connection
key_fact: In medicine, second brain commonly refers to the enteric nervous system in the gut, not a product concept.
notes: Used only to distinguish biological term from product framing.

title: Building a Second Brain overview
key_fact: The second-brain product concept is commonly framed around capture, organization, distillation, and later use/expression of knowledge.
notes: Not medical evidence; used to compare generic second-brain framing with medical adaptation.

title: Improving Diagnosis in Health Care
author_or_org: National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine
key_fact: Diagnosis is a collaborative process involving patients, families, clinicians, and health systems; diagnostic error is often system-related, not only individual clinician error.
notes: Core support for collaborative memory, workflow, monitoring, and feedback.

title: Patient-Centered Digital Health Records and Their Effects on Health Outcomes: Systematic Review
key_fact: Patient-centered digital records may improve engagement, self-management, knowledge, adherence, and some outcomes, but stronger evidence is still needed.
notes: Supports records access and patient engagement, but not passive portal-only design.

title: OpenNotes Patient Safety Toolkit
key_fact: Shared notes can support patient safety by improving transparency and enabling patients to identify errors or gaps.
notes: Used for patient correction loop and patient voice protection.

title: Association of patient portal access to notes with loop closure in primary care
author_or_org: Journal of the American Medical Informatics Association
topic_area: test/referral loop closure and patient portal note access
key_fact: Portal registration and reading notes were associated with higher odds of test/referral loop closure, but safety gaps remained.
notes: Supports follow-up loop tracking and patient access to notes.

title: OurNotes: Patients and Clinicians Creating Notes Together
key_fact: Patients can contribute previsit interval history and agendas to notes through portals.
notes: Supports patient story memory and previsit Health Thread updates.

title: OurDX: Patient and family contributions to diagnostic safety
key_fact: Patient and family previsit contributions can reveal diagnostic-process risks including repeated visits, access barriers, test/referral problems, care coordination issues, and communication breakdowns.
notes: Strong direct support for Shared Health Memory.

title: High quality patient records
key_fact: Problem lists can summarize past and present conditions and GP records can become longitudinal records, but problem-list quality varies and requires curation.
notes: Supports Health Threads as an improved problem-memory layer.

title: Closing the loop in test result communication
topic_area: test result follow-up and closed-loop communication
key_fact: Technology can help close communication loops, but responsibility, communication, and streamlined processes are essential.
notes: Core support for responsibility memory and pending-result ownership.

title: Missed imaging follow-up and diagnostic delay
key_fact: Failures to order or follow up diagnostic testing can contribute to diagnostic error and delay.
notes: Supports black-zone tracking for imaging follow-up.

title: Abnormal laboratory result follow-up program
key_fact: Failure to follow up lab tests is common; practical methods to reduce harm remain sparse.
notes: Supports abnormal-result ownership and follow-up tools.

title: Integrating patient-generated health data into EHRs: scoping review
key_fact: Integrating patient-generated health data into EHRs is important if clinicians are to use such data effectively.
notes: Supports selective ingestion and workflow integration.

title: Clinical responsibilities and patient-generated health data
key_fact: Clinicians need clear expectations, staffing models, workflows, escalation pathways, and clarity that some patient-generated data is not monitored in real time; trend views are preferred over isolated readings.
notes: Core safety support for avoiding raw data dumping.

title: Electronic patient record systems thematic review
topic_area: EPR safety and health IT risk
key_fact: Electronic patient record systems can improve care but can also contribute to patient safety incidents, missed care, delayed care, or incorrect care if poorly configured or implemented.
notes: Supports careful governance and workflow testing.

title: Digital clinical safety assurance
key_fact: Digital clinical safety assurance requires clinical risk management, hazard analysis, mitigation, documentation, and clinical safety accountability.
notes: Supports clinical safety case, governance, and clinician review requirements.

title: Clinical Decision Support Software guidance
key_fact: Some CDS functions may be excluded from device regulation, while software functions meeting device definitions remain subject to digital health policies; patient/caregiver-facing functions require careful consideration.
notes: Supports avoiding autonomous diagnosis and designing for transparency.

title: HL7 FHIR International Patient Access Implementation Guide
key_fact: FHIR IPA describes patient-facing access to problems, medications, immunizations, allergies, observations, lab results, notes, and documents using FHIR APIs.
notes: Supports interoperability layer concept.

title: SMART on FHIR documentation
key_fact: SMART on FHIR provides a practical framework for apps to connect with EHR and portal data using modern APIs.
notes: Supports implementation feasibility.

title: Patient Identification of Diagnostic Safety Blindspots through Shared Visit Notes
topic_area: shared notes and diagnostic safety blind spots
key_fact: Patient access to shared notes can support identification of diagnostic safety blind spots and good catches.
notes: Supports correction loop and patient voice as safety mechanism.

title: Global strategy on digital health 2020-2025
topic_area: digital health strategy and implementation capacity
key_fact: Digital health initiatives need appropriate financial, organizational, human, and technological resources, not only software.
notes: Supports low-resource and regional adaptation cautions.
